verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque vidisset Balaam quod placeret Domino ut benediceret Israëli, nequaquam abiit ut ante perrexerat, ut augurium quæreret : sed dirigens contra desertum vultum suum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣רְא בִּלְעָ֗ם כִּ֣י ט֞וֹב בְּעֵינֵ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ לְבָרֵ֣ךְ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וְלֹא־הָלַ֥ךְ כְּפַֽעַם־בְּפַ֖עַם לִקְרַ֣את נְחָשִׁ֑ים וַיָּ֥שֶׁת אֶל־הַמִּדְבָּ֖ר פָּנָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: And when Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord to bless Israel, he went not, as at other times, to seek for enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: et elevans oculos, vidit Israël in tentoriis commorantem per tribus suas : et irruente in se spiritu Dei,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֨א בִלְעָ֜ם אֶת־עֵינָ֗יו וַיַּרְא֙ אֶת־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל שֹׁכֵ֖ן לִשְׁבָטָ֑יו וַתְּהִ֥י עָלָ֖יו ר֥וּחַ אֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents according to their tribes; and the spirit of God came upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: assumpta parabola, ait :   Dixit Balaam filius Beor :  dixit homo, cujus obturatus est oculus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר נְאֻ֤ם בִּלְעָם֙ בְּנ֣וֹ בְעֹ֔ר וּנְאֻ֥ם הַגֶּ֖בֶר שְׁתֻ֥ם הָעָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit auditor sermonum Dei,  qui visionem Omnipotentis intuitus est,  qui cadit, et sic aperiuntur oculi ejus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נְאֻ֕ם שֹׁמֵ֖עַ אִמְרֵי־אֵ֑ל אֲשֶׁ֨ר מַחֲזֵ֤ה שַׁדַּי֙ יֶֽחֱזֶ֔ה נֹפֵ֖ל וּגְל֥וּי עֵינָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: He hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam pulchra tabernacula tua, Jacob,  et tentoria tua, Israël !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־טֹּ֥בוּ אֹהָלֶ֖יךָ יַעֲקֹ֑ב מִשְׁכְּנֹתֶ֖יךָ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: How goodly are thy tents, O Jacob, and thy tabernacles, O Israel!

verses.latin_vulgate: ut valles nemorosæ,  ut horti juxta fluvios irrigui,  ut tabernacula quæ fixit Dominus,  quasi cedri prope aquas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּנְחָלִ֣ים נִטָּ֔יוּ כְּגַנֹּ֖ת עֲלֵ֣י נָהָ֑ר כַּאֲהָלִים֙ נָטַ֣ע יְהֹוָ֔ה כַּאֲרָזִ֖ים עֲלֵי־מָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: As the valleys are they spread forth, as gardens by the river’s side, as the trees of lign aloes which the Lord hath planted, and as cedar trees beside the waters.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fluet aqua de situla ejus,  et semen illius erit in aquas multas.  Tolletur propter Agag, rex ejus,  et auferetur regnum illius.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִֽזַּל־מַ֙יִם֙ מִדָּ֣לְיָ֔ו וְזַרְע֖וֹ בְּמַ֣יִם רַבִּ֑ים וְיָרֹ֤ם מֵֽאֲגַג֙ מַלְכּ֔וֹ וְתִנַּשֵּׂ֖א מַלְכֻתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: He shall pour the water out of his buckets, and his seed shall be in many waters, and his king shall be higher than Agag, and his kingdom shall be exalted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus eduxit illum de Ægypto,  cujus fortitudo similis est rhinocerotis.  Devorabunt gentes hostes illius,  ossaque eorum confringent, et perforabunt sagittis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֚ל מוֹצִיא֣וֹ מִמִּצְרַ֔יִם כְּתוֹעֲפֹ֥ת רְאֵ֖ם ל֑וֹ יֹאכַ֞ל גּוֹיִ֣ם צָרָ֗יו וְעַצְמֹתֵיהֶ֛ם יְגָרֵ֖ם וְחִצָּ֥יו יִמְחָֽץ׃
verses.king_james: God brought him forth out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an unicorn: he shall eat up the nations his enemies, and shall break their bones, and pierce them through with his arrows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accubans dormivit ut leo,  et quasi leæna, quam suscitare nullus audebit.  Qui benedixerit tibi, erit et ipse benedictus :  qui maledixerit, in maledictione reputabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כָּרַ֨ע שָׁכַ֧ב כַּאֲרִ֛י וּכְלָבִ֖יא מִ֣י יְקִימֶ֑נּוּ מְבָרְכֶ֣יךָ בָר֔וּךְ וְאֹרְרֶ֖יךָ אָרֽוּר׃
verses.king_james: He couched, he lay down as a lion, and as a great lion: who shall stir him up? Blessed is he that blesseth thee, and cursed is he that curseth thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Iratusque Balac contra Balaam, complosis manibus ait : Ad maledicendum inimicis meis vocavi te, quibus e contrario tertio benedixisti :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּֽחַר־אַ֤ף בָּלָק֙ אֶל־בִּלְעָ֔ם וַיִּסְפֹּ֖ק אֶת־כַּפָּ֑יו וַיֹּ֨אמֶר בָּלָ֜ק אֶל־בִּלְעָ֗ם לָקֹ֤ב אֹֽיְבַי֙ קְרָאתִ֔יךָ וְהִנֵּה֙ בֵּרַ֣כְתָּ בָרֵ֔ךְ זֶ֖ה שָׁלֹ֥שׁ פְּעָמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And Balak’s anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together: and Balak said unto Balaam, I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them these three times.

verses.latin_vulgate: revertere ad locum tuum. Decreveram quidem magnifice honorare te, sed Dominus privavit te honore disposito.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֖ה בְּרַח־לְךָ֣ אֶל־מְקוֹמֶ֑ךָ אָמַ֙רְתִּי֙ כַּבֵּ֣ד אֲכַבֶּדְךָ֔ וְהִנֵּ֛ה מְנָעֲךָ֥ יְהֹוָ֖ה מִכָּבֽוֹד׃
verses.king_james: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honour; but, lo, the Lord hath kept thee back from honour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Balaam ad Balac : Nonne nuntiis tuis, quos misisti ad me, dixi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר בִּלְעָ֖ם אֶל־בָּלָ֑ק הֲלֹ֗א גַּ֧ם אֶל־מַלְאָכֶ֛יךָ אֲשֶׁר־שָׁלַ֥חְתָּ אֵלַ֖י דִּבַּ֥רְתִּי לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: And Balaam said unto Balak, Spake I not also to thy messengers which thou sentest unto me, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Si dederit mihi Balac plenam domum suam argenti et auri, non potero præterire sermonem Domini Dei mei, ut vel boni quid vel mali proferam ex corde meo : sed quidquid Dominus dixerit, hoc loquar ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יִתֶּן־לִ֨י בָלָ֜ק מְלֹ֣א בֵיתוֹ֮ כֶּ֣סֶף וְזָהָב֒ לֹ֣א אוּכַ֗ל לַעֲבֹר֙ אֶת־פִּ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת טוֹבָ֛ה א֥וֹ רָעָ֖ה מִלִּבִּ֑י אֲשֶׁר־יְדַבֵּ֥ר יְהֹוָ֖ה אֹת֥וֹ אֲדַבֵּֽר׃
verses.king_james: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the commandment of the Lord, to do either good or bad of mine own mind; but what the Lord saith, that will I speak?

verses.latin_vulgate: verumtamen pergens ad populum meum, dabo consilium, quid populus tuus populo huic faciat extremo tempore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֕ה הִנְנִ֥י הוֹלֵ֖ךְ לְעַמִּ֑י לְכָה֙ אִיעָ֣צְךָ֔ אֲשֶׁ֨ר יַעֲשֶׂ֜ה הָעָ֥ם הַזֶּ֛ה לְעַמְּךָ֖ בְּאַחֲרִ֥ית הַיָּמִֽים׃
verses.king_james: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come therefore, and I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sumpta igitur parabola, rursum ait :   Dixit Balaam filius Beor :  dixit homo, cujus obturatus est oculus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר נְאֻ֤ם בִּלְעָם֙ בְּנ֣וֹ בְעֹ֔ר וּנְאֻ֥ם הַגֶּ֖בֶר שְׁתֻ֥ם הָעָֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:

verses.latin_vulgate: dixit auditor sermonum Dei,  qui novit doctrinam Altissimi,  et visiones Omnipotentis videt,  qui cadens apertos habet oculos :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נְאֻ֗ם שֹׁמֵ֙עַ֙ אִמְרֵי־אֵ֔ל וְיֹדֵ֖עַ דַּ֣עַת עֶלְי֑וֹן מַחֲזֵ֤ה שַׁדַּי֙ יֶֽחֱזֶ֔ה נֹפֵ֖ל וּגְל֥וּי עֵינָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: He hath said, which heard the words of God, and knew the knowledge of the most High, which saw the vision of the Almighty, falling into a trance, but having his eyes open:

verses.latin_vulgate: Videbo eum, sed non modo :  intuebor illum, sed non prope.  Orietur stella ex Jacob,  et consurget virga de Israël :  et percutiet duces Moab,  vastabitque omnes filios Seth.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶרְאֶ֙נּוּ֙ וְלֹ֣א עַתָּ֔ה אֲשׁוּרֶ֖נּוּ וְלֹ֣א קָר֑וֹב דָּרַ֨ךְ כּוֹכָ֜ב מִֽיַּעֲקֹ֗ב וְקָ֥ם שֵׁ֙בֶט֙ מִיִּשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וּמָחַץ֙ פַּאֲתֵ֣י מוֹאָ֔ב וְקַרְקַ֖ר כׇּל־בְּנֵי־שֵֽׁת׃
verses.king_james: I shall see him, but not now: I shall behold him, but not nigh: there shall come a Star out of Jacob, and a Sceptre shall rise out of Israel, and shall smite the corners of Moab, and destroy all the children of Sheth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit Idumæa possessio ejus :  hæreditas Seir cedet inimicis suis :  Israël vero fortiter aget.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֨ה אֱד֜וֹם יְרֵשָׁ֗ה וְהָיָ֧ה יְרֵשָׁ֛ה שֵׂעִ֖יר אֹיְבָ֑יו וְיִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל עֹ֥שֶׂה חָֽיִל׃
verses.king_james: And Edom shall be a possession, Seir also shall be a possession for his enemies; and Israel shall do valiantly.

verses.latin_vulgate: De Jacob erit qui dominetur,  et perdat reliquias civitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֵ֖רְדְּ מִֽיַּעֲקֹ֑ב וְהֶֽאֱבִ֥יד שָׂרִ֖יד מֵעִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: Out of Jacob shall come he that shall have dominion, and shall destroy him that remaineth of the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque vidisset Amalec, assumens parabolam, ait :  Principium gentium Amalec,  cujus extrema perdentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּרְא֙ אֶת־עֲמָלֵ֔ק וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר רֵאשִׁ֤ית גּוֹיִם֙ עֲמָלֵ֔ק וְאַחֲרִית֖וֹ עֲדֵ֥י אֹבֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: And when he looked on Amalek, he took up his parable, and said, Amalek was the first of the nations; but his latter end shall be that he perish for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidit quoque Cinæum : et assumpta parabola, ait :  Robustum quidem est habitaculum tuum :  sed si in petra posueris nidum tuum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּרְא֙ אֶת־הַקֵּינִ֔י וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר אֵיתָן֙ מֽוֹשָׁבֶ֔ךָ וְשִׂ֥ים בַּסֶּ֖לַע קִנֶּֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: And he looked on the Kenites, and took up his parable, and said, Strong is thy dwellingplace, and thou puttest thy nest in a rock.

verses.latin_vulgate: et fueris electus de stirpe Cin,  quamdiu poteris permanere ? Assur enim capiet te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֥י אִם־יִהְיֶ֖ה לְבָ֣עֵֽר קָ֑יִן עַד־מָ֖ה אַשּׁ֥וּר תִּשְׁבֶּֽךָּ׃
verses.king_james: Nevertheless the Kenite shall be wasted, until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Assumptaque parabola iterum locutus est :  Heu ! quis victurus est,  quando ista faciet Deus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשָּׂ֥א מְשָׁל֖וֹ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר א֕וֹי מִ֥י יִחְיֶ֖ה מִשֻּׂמ֥וֹ אֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: And he took up his parable, and said, Alas, who shall live when God doeth this!

verses.latin_vulgate: Venient in trieribus de Italia :  superabunt Assyrios, vastabuntque Hebræos, et ad extremum etiam ipsi peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְצִים֙ מִיַּ֣ד כִּתִּ֔ים וְעִנּ֥וּ אַשּׁ֖וּר וְעִנּוּ־עֵ֑בֶר וְגַם־ה֖וּא עֲדֵ֥י אֹבֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: And ships shall come from the coast of Chittim, and shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber, and he also shall perish for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Surrexitque Balaam, et reversus est in locum suum : Balac quoque via, qua venerat, rediit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣קׇם בִּלְעָ֔ם וַיֵּ֖לֶךְ וַיָּ֣שׇׁב לִמְקֹמ֑וֹ וְגַם־בָּלָ֖ק הָלַ֥ךְ לְדַרְכּֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place: and Balak also went his way.
